findings.description: subprocess.run with shell=True and an f-string built from unsanitized job.payload values is a textbook command injection (e.g. source='x; rm -rf /').
attempt is reset to 0 every iteration before being incremented, so the while attempt < MAX_RETRIES guard never advances — run_with_retries loops forever on persistent failure and never returns False.
async poll_loop calls blocking time.sleep() (here and via run_with_retries), freezing the entire event loop for every idle poll and retry backoff instead of awaiting asyncio.sleep().
Exponential backoff is broken: because attempt is always 0 at the sleep, RETRY_BACKOFF_SECONDS * (attempt + 1) is permanently 2.0s — the variable name promises growth it never delivers.
Bare except Exception swallows all errors and retries blindly, treating non-retryable failures (bad payload, ValueError) identically to transient ones.
pop(0) on a list is O(n) per dequeue because every remaining element shifts; a queue should use collections.deque for O(1) pops.
stop_after compares cumulative self.processed + self.failed instead of jobs handled in this call, so reusing the worker makes a later poll_loop terminate prematurely.
convert captures stdout/stderr then discards them, returning only False on non-zero exit with zero diagnostics for debugging failures.
asyncio is imported but never used (the one async function blocks on time.sleep instead of asyncio.sleep), making the import dead weight.
